name: 액트프러너(Act-Preneur): 실행 중심의 창업가 육성 철학

shapes.text: For entrepreneurs, who are more familiar with hearing "no" than "yes" and more accustomed to rejection than approval, the nickname "underdog" suits them better than "top dog.“Entrepreneurs are those who believe they can achieve what others say is impossible, taking action to bring into existence what does not yet exist.Inspired by the term "underdog," which refers to a team or player with low chances of winning in sports, underdogs was founded with the belief that even the smallest possibilities can spark social innovation and create transformative change in the world through business. It is a collective of underdog entrepreneurs determined to flip the script and build a better world.Underdogs is a specialized institution for entrepreneurship education, dedicated to discovering and nurturing social innovation entrepreneurs both in Korea and abroad. Since its establishment in 2015, it has fostered over 20,000 social innovation entrepreneurs.
UNDERDOG: The underdog in a competition that is expected to lose rather than win.
An idea becomes an item (solution) when it is accompanied by an action.
[continuously solves problems through action and transforms the world ]
An entrepreneur who solves problems through execution and transforms the world.
Entrepreneurs with fundamentals:quickly respond to changes and problem.Make decisions based on customer perspectives and insights.Grow by achieving clear outcomes, even if they are small
Someone who creates businesses between people who buy and sell goods
Someonewho continuously solves problems through action and transforms the world
Introduction to the 7 Steps of Act-Preneur
How to gather the right team How to work well with teamHow to define direction
How to analyze the problemHow to find cause of the problemHow to analyze related
How to discover solutions (items)How to benchmarkHow to prioritize items
How to define customersHow to create revenue modelsHow to find a business model
Validating customersBuilding MVP products/servicesTesting minimum viable features
How to systemize business operationsHR, Sales, Marketing
How to generate salesHow to secure investments and funding
STEP 1: Team Building"Understand team members’ tendencies, designate a leader, and establish shared goals and principles of collaboration."
STEP 2: Define a problem"Design the solution direction by clearly identifying the core problem of entrepreneurship through comprehensive analysis."
STEP 3:Deriving Item"Generate and validate creative entrepreneurial ideas to solve the team’s problems by referring to diverse examples."
STEP 4: Item Concretization"Develop problem-solving ideas into a customer-centric business model and design strategies focused on the target market."

During the bootcamp, expert course managers participate as team advisors, providing feedback and practical execution directions.Over the course of 4 days, participants systematically practice essential entrepreneurship components such as team building, problem definition, idea generation, and idea refinement (completing the Act-Canvas).The program consists of 30% lectures and 70% workshops, offering a hands-on approach where teams collaborate to concretely develop entrepreneurial ideas.

Whatis Course Manager ?  A guide who will navigate you through the boot camp!What does a Course Manager do?  Delivers lectures to help you progress through the boot camp.  Assists you in completing your worksheets effectively.  Answers any questions you have during the training.  Solves any issues that arise in real time!

Turn on your camera so everyone can see each other.Ask questions without hesitation.Listen to the lecture and do your best to complete the worksheet.Give your best effort and enjoy the entire process!
Let’s transform the world through entrepreneurship and become Act-Preneurs who can create value through sustainable execution."
Introduction to the 7 Steps of Act-Preneur
Generate sales within 6 months! Reach  at least 1 Million USD in 2 years!
How to find team members who fit your visionHow to work well as a teamHow to set the team’s direction
How to explore the problemHow to find the root causeHow to analyze key stakeholders

WHYWhy does ud. prioritize team-based entrepreneurship?
Why does ud. prefer team-based startups?Having teammates provides thepower to keep goingand it allows for us to solve multiple problems
WHYHow does the team effectively test and improve multiple problems at the same time?

How does the team effectively test and improve multiple problems at the same time?Team members need to work as one, moving toward the same goal.For this to work, the team needs clear working rules (rules) & a shared direction (mission and vision) to effectively test and improve multiple problems at once.
A good team has one strong leader who helps the team work together and take action efficiently.This allows the team to test and improve multiple problems efficiently, leading to better results.
Understand both your own and your team members’ tendencies, appoint a strong leader to guide execution, and build a sustainable team working toward shared goals.
WHYWhat is the D.O.G.S Personality Test?
Based on the DISC model developed by Dr. William Moulton Marston at Columbia University.
A diagnostic tool for team-building tailored for entrepreneurs
Developed using data from 20,000 entrepreneurs in collaboration with psychology experts to create a systematic team-building solution.
(1) D.O.G.S workshop  -> Check Personality -> Select Leader(2) Establishment of MVC&G

Get to know each other’s personality through the D.O.G.S workshop ,
Please choose a leader for our team.

Teammates NameExpected Team Members PersonalityActual Team Members PersonalityLeader Name / Reason for ChoiceJason(D) Beagle(D) DobermanJason[Because of the fast momentum]Jethrow(D) Doberman(D) BeagleJulie(G) Saint Bernard(D) BeagleMatt(O) Border Collie(O) Border Collie

The Duty of the Leader1. [Decisiveness] When unsure, make a quick decision.2. [Leadership] When you see a team member who is disengaged, immediately engage them and make them participate.3. [Deliverables] Deliver workshop outcomes on time, no matter what.4. [Direction] Inspires team members' enthusiasm and keeps them focused on the goal.

CategoryExplanationExampleMissionYour team's current goalsNurture entrepreneurs who can create impact in the world.VisionWhat the future looks like for your teamAs a pacemaker, work alongside entrepreneurs - a company that makes a difference in the world.Core-valueWhat values do your team care about?Timely delivery, communication, and attitude.Ground rulesRules team members should follow when collaboratingCustomer-centric thinking.
Communicate clearly.
Have an action-oriented attitude.
enhancing urban transportation through innovative solutions.

Traditional taxi services often suffer from inefficiencies and limited accessibility, leading to long wait times and unpredictable pricing for passengers. Our goal is to address these challenges by providing a reliable and user-friendly ride-sharing platform that seamlessly connects passengers with drivers.

CategoryContentMission"We reimagine the way the world moves for the better."Vision"Transportation as reliable as running water, everywhere for everyone."Core-valueGo Get It: Embrace a champion's mindset, overcoming adversity to achieve our mission. Trip Obsessed: Focus on creating exceptional experiences in our marketplace.Build with Heart: Care deeply about our impact and strive to make a positive difference. Stand for Safety: Embed safety into everything we do, continuously improving for our communitiesGround rulesRespect Everyone – Speak politely and listen actively.Prioritize Safety – Drive carefully and always wear a seatbelt.Follow Laws – Obey traffic rules and avoid distractions.Keep It Clean – Remove trash and maintain a tidy vehicle.
tech-driven hospitality chain that partners with hotels to provide standardized, affordable, and quality

The budget hotel industry faces challenges in maintaining consistent quality, cleanliness, and affordability. Travelers often struggle with unpredictable service standards, while small hotel owners lack the resources and technology to optimize their occupancy.

CategoryContentMissionOYO is a global platform that empowers entrepreneurs and small businesses with hotels and homes by providing full-stack technology that increases earnings and eases operations, bringing affordable and trusted accommodation that guests can book instantly.Vision"To become the world's largest and most preferred hotel chain."Core-valueCustomer-Centricity: Placing guests at the heart of all decisions to ensure exceptional experiences.Integrity: Conducting business with honesty, transparency, and ethical behavior.Innovation: Continuously seeking new ways to improve services and offerings.Ground rulesRespect Everyone – Treat guests, partners, and teammates with kindness.Deliver Quality – Maintain high standards at all OYO properties.Be Honest – Act with transparency in all business dealings.

<Learning Objectives>1. Understand diverse problems through personal and societal perspectives.2. Evaluate problems based on seriousness and timing to determine priority issues.3. Identify and analyze diverse stakeholdersinvolved in the problem.4. Define the core problem we aim to solve in a single sentence.
Business opportunities arise from diverse societal problems!The severity of a problem (pain point) determines whether customers will invest time or money to solve it.
A structured process to identify problems and analyze their root causes
A structured approach to identifying problems, setting priorities, and determining the problems we genuinely want to solve
WHYThe Process of Selecting and Defining Problems
Discovering social issues starting from personal problems.
Discovering social issues by empathizing with external problems
Strong motivation to solve the problem Deep understanding of the problem.
Risk of focusing only on personal concerns.
Can include widely recognized issues. Likely to select issues many people empathize with.
Risk of low motivation or understanding for issues that feel personally irrelevant
How to Select Problems: Personal Issues vs. Socially Significant Issues
The IF Matrix evaluates business ideas based on four key factors: Financial Impact, Social Impact, Capability, and Environment
How big is the problem? How many people are affected?
How serious is the problem? What long-term effects does it have?
How is this problem connected to today’s trends and policies?
Do people care about this problem? Does it get public support?
Over 1 billion people don’t have clean drinking water (Water.org).
If kids don’t get an education, they stay in poverty (Khan Academy).
More natural disasters are making governments focus on clean energy (Tesla).
Many people donate to food banks to help the hungry (Feeding America).
Using Seriousness and Timing to determine which issues to focus on
Identify personal and social problems by reflecting on your own experiences through the Inside Out approach

Are others around me experiencing similar problems?Is solving this problem worth the investment?What challenges or inconveniences does this issue create?Does this problem require urgent attention?Have you identified the root cause of the problem?What changes will occur when the problem is solved?
Write down a Situation That Made Me Uncomfortable and the Problem I Found inthe situation
Describe whether the problem I experienced is also being faced by people around me and whether it resonates with a larger audience on a societal level
Do people care about this problem? Does it get public support?
How is this problem connected to today’s trends and policies?
How serious is the problem? What long-term effects does it have?
How big is the problem? How many people are affected?
HOWTips for Writing an I.O Workshop

✅ 1. Defining the Problem (My Problem)→ Clearly articulate the specific pain point you are experiencing.→ The problem should not just be an inconvenience but something that must be addressed.✅ 2. Identifying the Social Impact (Why It’s a Social Issue?)→ Analyze how this issue extends beyond personal inconvenience to affect society.→ Expand the problem from an individual concern to a broader social issue. ✅ 3. Use Checklist(Check List)→ Identify whether others around you face the same issue.→ Assess whether people are willing to pay for a solution to this problem.→ Determine the root cause of the problem and predict potential challenges in implementing a solution.

📌 " The best way to solve a problem is to deeply understand it from the perspective of those affected."→ Use an ‘Inside-Out’ approach to connect personal problems to broader social issues, reinforcing the necessity of finding a solution! 🚀
enhancing urban transportation through innovative solutions.

Traditional taxi services often suffer from inefficiencies and limited accessibility, leading to long wait times and unpredictable pricing for passengers. Our goal is to address these challenges by providing a reliable and user-friendly ride-sharing platform that seamlessly connects passengers with drivers.
Identify personal and social problems by reflecting on your own experiences through the Inside Out approach

One night, I was struggling to find a taxi after a long day. I kept waiting, but none were available. When I finally got one, the fare was unexpectedly high, and the driver took a longer route. I felt frustrated by the lack of transparency, reliability, and convenience in taxi services.

Limited access to transportation, especially in certain areas or late hoursLack of pricing transparency and inconsistent service qualityNo alternative to traditional taxis, restricting consumer choice
Many people complain about taxi issues online.The rise of the sharing economy made ride-hailing more acceptable.
Taxi rules were outdated and didn’t support new mobility solutions.Many cities needed better transport options but lacked alternatives.
People lose time and money due to unreliable taxis.A lack of good transport options affects jobs and personal safety.
Millions of people need reliable rides every day.Many face difficulties getting a taxi, especially at night or in remote areas.

Are others around me experiencing similar problems?Is solving this problem worth the investment?What challenges or inconveniences does this issue create?Does this problem require urgent attention?Have you identified the root cause of the problem?will large changes occur when the problem is solved?
tech-driven hospitality chain that partners with hotels to provide standardized, affordable, and quality

The budget hotel industry faces challenges in maintaining consistent quality, cleanliness, and affordability. Travelers often struggle with unpredictable service standards, while small hotel owners lack the resources and technology to optimize their occupancy.
Identify personal and social problems by reflecting on your own experiences through the Inside Out approach

Are others around me experiencing similar problems?Is solving this problem worth the investment?What challenges or inconveniences does this issue create?Does this problem require urgent attention?Have you identified the root cause of the problem?will large changes occur when the problem is solved?

I struggled to find budget-friendly, clean, and reliable hotels. Many affordable options lacked basic amenities and standard quality. I wondered—why isn’t there a way to ensure affordable, standardized lodging?

Budget travelers lack access to reliable and clean hotels.Unbranded hotels often have inconsistent service and poor maintenance.Travelers face difficulty booking affordable accommodations with guaranteed quality.
Budget travel is growing, increasing demand for standardized hotels.More travelers want cost-effective, quality-assured accommodations.
Lack of quality assurance in budget hotels creates distrust.Fragmented hotel markets make it hard for customers to find reliable options.
Travelers waste time searching for reliable and clean budget hotels.Poor lodging experiences affect customer satisfaction and tourism growth.
500M+ budget travelers seek affordable accommodations annually.80% of independent hotels lack standardized service quality.
반려동물 사료에 포함된 인공 첨가물과 보존제가 반려동물의 건강에 부정적인 영향을 미치고 있습니다
"Write about social issues that you are interested in or have heard about."
Describe how this issue is connected to me or how it affects me and those around me. explain the root causes of the problem
Analyze external problems and explore their societal roots using the Outside-In perspective to uncover meaningful and impactful solutions.
"Summarize the problem we aim to solve in a single sentence."
Do people care about this problem? Does it get public support?
How is this problem connected to today’s trends and policies?
How serious is the problem? What long-term effects does it have?
How big is the problem? How many people are affected?
Uber is a global mobility platform that provides app-based ride-hailing and delivery services.

Uber operates on a platform intermediary model, connecting drivers and passengers through an on-demand mobility service. Its revenue comes from a commission taken from the fare paid by passengers. Additionally, Uber diversifies its revenue streams through services like food delivery (Uber Eats) and freight transportation (Uber Freight).
반려동물 사료에 포함된 인공 첨가물과 보존제가 반려동물의 건강에 부정적인 영향을 미치고 있습니다

Our ProblemTraditional taxi services are inefficient, expensive, and difficult to access, leading to a demand for alternative transportation solutions.Root Causes:Cost & Availability: Traditional taxis are expensive, and availability is often inconsistent.Technology Gap: Many traditional taxi services lack real-time tracking and efficient dispatching.Regulatory Restrictions: Taxi industry regulations limit competition and hinder service improvements.
Analyze external problems and explore their societal roots using the Outside-In perspective to uncover meaningful and impactful solutions.
Create a tech-driven ride-sharing platform that enhances accessibility, affordability, and efficiency in urban transportation

Long wait times and high fares create frustration among commuters.Consumers desire seamless, safe, and cost-effective transportation alternatives.Drivers seek flexible job opportunities with fair earning potential.
Heavy regulation and opposition from traditional taxi operators.\Safety concerns and trust issues in ride-sharing services.Environmental impact of increased vehicle usage.
Poor urban mobility can lead to economic inefficiency, missed opportunities, and increased environmental polution.Limited transport access reduces quality of life for lower-income populations.
Millions of people in cities worldwide rely on taxis and public transportation.Rapid urbanization increases the demand for flexible and affordable transit solutions.
OYO Rooms is a global hospitality platform that partners with budget hotels to provide standardized and affordable accommodations.

OYO Rooms operates on a franchise and aggregation model, partnering with budget hotels to standardize services and improve occupancy rates. It generates revenue by charging franchise fees and a commission on bookings made through its platform. By leveraging technology, OYO optimizes hotel operations, enhances customer experience, and expands its global presence.
반려동물 사료에 포함된 인공 첨가물과 보존제가 반려동물의 건강에 부정적인 영향을 미치고 있습니다

Our ProblemBudget-conscious travelers and business professionals struggle to find affordable, standardized, and quality accommodations, especially in emerging markets and smaller cities.Root Causes:Lack of Standardization: Inconsistent service quality in budget hotels.High Cost of Quality Stays: Affordable options often lack cleanliness and security.Limited Availability: Major hotel chains focus on metropolitan areas.Fragmented Hospitality Industry: Independent hotels lack branding and visibility.
Analyze external problems and explore their societal roots using the Outside-In perspective to uncover meaningful and impactful solutions.
Create a technology-driven hospitality platform that standardizes budget accommodations, enhances hotel visibility, and ensures quality, affordability, and accessibility for travelers worldwide.
Homeowners struggle with mortgage payments and seek additional income sources.Tourists desire affordable, authentic stays that allow them to experience local culture.
Government regulations and zoning laws limit the adoption of home-sharing solutions.Trust and safety concerns between hosts and guests require mitigation strategies.
Housing shortages can lead to homelessness and social displacement. The inefficiency of space usage in cities contributes to urban sprawl and environmental concerns.
Millions of people globally face housing affordability issues in urban areas.Tourism-dependent economies require scalable lodging solutions to accommodate visitors.
HOW Outside In – ex. OYO rooms
